compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
The tickets-branch push-retry logic resolves a non-fast-forward push with a "stash → merge → pop" dance, but the `git stash pop` is run **unconditionally and its return code is never checked**. When the stashed working-tree copy of a tracked bridge-state file (`.bridge_state/prev_snapshot.json`, `.bridge_state/bindings.json`) conflicts with the freshly-merged upstream copy, the pop leaves **git conflict markers in the working tree and an unmerged (`UU`) index entry**. Nothing detects or repairs this, so the corruption is silently committed/left behind and wedges two subsystems:
- **Reconcile**: the read-side guard fail-closes every pass ("prev_snapshot.json is corrupt or contains git conflict markers … Aborting reconcile pass").
- **All store writes**: `git commit` refuses to commit with an unmerged path, surfacing to users as `git commit failed while holding lock` on any `rebar` write (claim/edit/transition/archive/…).

Observed live this session: a concurrent reconciler push-retry left `.bridge_state/prev_snapshot.json` with `<<<<<<< Updated upstream` / `>>>>>>> Stashed changes` markers (stages 1/2/3 in the index). HEAD’s committed copy was clean — the corruption was purely the un-popped stash conflict in the worktree+index. `rebar archive` failed with `git commit failed while holding lock` until I restored the file to HEAD.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Have a local tickets worktree with modified, tracked `.bridge_state/*` files (normal reconciler state) AND an `origin/tickets` that is ahead and also touched those same files.
2. Trigger a write that auto-pushes (any `rebar` write) so the non-fast-forward push-retry path runs: merge fails "dirty working tree" → `git stash push` → `git merge` (updates `.bridge_state/prev_snapshot.json` from upstream) → `git stash pop`.
3. The pop conflicts on `prev_snapshot.json` (both sides changed it).

**Expected:** the push-retry either resolves the bridge-state file deterministically (those files are reconciler-regenerable) or fails cleanly, leaving the worktree/index in a consistent, writable, reconcile-able state.

**Actual:** conflict markers + an unmerged `UU` index entry are left in `prev_snapshot.json`. Reconcile fail-closes on every subsequent pass, and every `rebar` store write fails with `git commit failed while holding lock`. Manual recovery is required (restore the file to HEAD, or delete it to force a full re-fetch).

## Root Cause
`src/rebar/_store/push.py:158-180` — the dirty-working-tree branch (bug 12a6): `git stash push` (160), `git merge` post-stash (166-173), then `git stash pop --quiet` at **line 174 with the comment "pop unconditionally"**. The pop’s return code is never inspected; a conflicting pop applies-with-conflict, keeps the stash, and leaves markers + an unmerged index. The `merge2.returncode` check (175) aborts only `merge2`, not a conflicted pop, and the clean-`merge2` path just `continue`s — so a successful merge2 + conflicting pop produces silent corruption.

Downstream guards behave correctly but only as fail-closed detectors, not repair:
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:733-785` aborts the pass on a corrupt/conflict-marked `prev_snapshot.json` (SAFETY INVARIANT) — correct, but leaves reconcile wedged with only a manual-recovery message.
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/binding_store.py:104` is the same corruption class for `bindings.json`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The push-retry stash-pop checks its result; a conflicting pop is detected and resolved (or the operation fails cleanly) — never silently leaving conflict markers / an unmerged index in `.bridge_state/*`.
- [ ] Because `.bridge_state/*` are reconciler-regenerable, the resolution should deterministically take one side (e.g. reset those paths to the merged HEAD / `--theirs`) and drop the stash, rather than leaving an unmerged entry.
- [ ] After a push-retry that hit a bridge-state conflict, the tickets worktree is left writable (no `UU`) and reconcile-able (valid JSON), with no manual intervention.
- [ ] A store write that encounters a pre-existing unmerged `.bridge_state/*` index entry self-heals (or fails with an actionable, non-cryptic error) instead of the bare `git commit failed while holding lock`.
- [ ] Regression test simulating a stash-pop conflict on `prev_snapshot.json` during push-retry asserts the worktree/index end consistent and both reconcile + the next store write succeed.

## Affected Files (initial)
- `src/rebar/_store/push.py` (`_push_tickets_branch` retry loop, stash→merge→pop)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py` (prev_snapshot read guard — consider auto-recovery)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/binding_store.py` (same corruption class for bindings.json)
- `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` (the commit-under-lock path that surfaces the unmerged-index failure)

file_impact.reason: stash->merge->pop dance; unconditional unchecked stash pop (root cause)
prev_snapshot read guard fail-closes; candidate for auto-recovery

compiled_state.title: Push-retry stash-pop leaves conflict markers in .bridge_state/*, wedging reconcile (fail-closed) AND all store writes
